title: cordia bullata subsp. humilis
intro: Cordia bullata subsp. humilis, also known as the "Cape Leadwood", is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the southern and western parts of South Africa. Traditionally, the plant is used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, and has been reported to have various health benefits such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and improving digestion. Cordia bullata subsp. humilis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
